svg.text: VIETNAMESE
TONAL PHONOLOGY
SIX TONES OF VIETNAMESE
ngang: level mid tone (a) — flat and sustained
huyền: falling tone (à) — low falling
sắc: rising tone (á) — high rising
hỏi: dipping tone (ả) — mid-low dip then rise
ngã: creaky-rising (ã) — rising with glottalization
nặng: stopped (ạ) — low, short, glottal stop final
ma mà má mả mã mạ = 6 different words!
CONSONANT SYSTEM
Initial: b d đ g gi kh l m n nh ng ngh ph qu r s t th tr v x
đ /ɗ/ = implosive (pulled-in d) • gi /z/ = palatal fricative
Final: -c -ch -ng -nh -p -t -m -n only (no voiced finals)
VOWEL SYSTEM
14 vowel nuclei; 3 diphthongs; relatively complex for SE Asia
/ɯ/ (unrounded u) unique to Vietnamese in SE Asian context
ZHUANG PHONOLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
ZHUANG TONES (6-8)
Standard Zhuang: 6 tones (some dialects 8)
Tone 1 (high level) • Tone 2 (mid rising) • Tone 3 (mid falling)
Tone 4 (low falling) • Tone 5 (low rising) • Tone 6 (mid level)
Tone 7 + 8: checked tones (syllable-final stop closure)
ZHUANG CONSONANTS
Initial consonants: p b m f v d t n l g k ng h r s z
Prenasalized stops: mb nd mg (like Bantu! different origin)
No complex consonant clusters at syllable onset
SHARED SE ASIAN TONAL FEATURES
Both: monosyllabic roots • both: tonal systems (6+ tones)
Both: simple syllable structure CV(C) • no consonant clusters
Vietnamese: Austroasiatic family • Zhuang: Tai-Kadai family
Converged phonologically through Southeast Asian Mainland sprachbund
PLATE 38b ALT · VIETNAMESE / ZHUANG · PHONOLOGY
